title: Lively Portuguese Ovoli Mushroom Soup
description: A tangy, shaggy soup that comforts sick days with a burst of Portuguese flavor, featuring ovoli mushrooms poached for silky texture.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, sliced carrot, and celery, sautéing until onion becomes translucent.
Stir in sliced ovoli mushrooms and cook for 3 minutes until they release moisture.
Pour in white wine and let it reduce by half, scraping up browned bits.
Add vegetable broth, chopped thyme, smoked paprika, and lemon zest, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Use an immersion blender to mash the soup to a shaggy texture, or transfer to a blender in batches.
Return soup to the pot and stir to incorporate the blended mixture, adjusting consistency with a splash of broth if needed.
Taste and season with a pinch of salt if desired, keeping the low-sodium intent in mind.
Ladle the hot soup into bowls, garnish with a drizzle of olive oil, and serve immediately.
